ATENEO SCHOOL OF MEDICINE AND PUBLIC HEALTH

BAMM BUSINESS VENTURES INC.

A Strategic Management Paper — STRAMA Defense

Presented by: Mary Joy B. Ong

ASMPH · AY 2026–2027

COMPANY BACKGROUND
Overview of BAMM Business Ventures Inc.
Corporate Profile
  • Established: March 7, 2016 in Aurora Province
  • Parent Group: IDC Group of Companies - Industrial Development Corporation (MWI · MCS · Mejore · BAMM BVI)
  • PCAB License: Category B (Maximum contract ceiling: ₱75M)
  • Workforce: ~80 full-time + ~150 project-based laborers
  • Core Operations: Horizontal construction (roads, bridges, flood control), vertical construction, and heavy equipment rental.
  • Exclusive Asset: Owns a sustainable sand and gravel quarry in Casiguran, Aurora.
Revenue Growth (2022-2024)
YearGross RevenueYoY Growth
2022₱59.77M—
2023₱73.80M+23%
2024 (Actual)₱93.01M+26%

* Net Profit Margin improving steadily, reaching 8.26% in 2024.

VISION STATEMENT
Critique and Strategy-Aligned Proposed Vision
Current Vision

"Developing with future generations in mind."

Proposed Vision

"To be the leading provider4 of sustainable quarrying and infrastructure development2 within Central Luzon3, creating long-lasting value for communities and future generations1 by 20405."

MISSION STATEMENT
Alignment with David's 10 Components
Current Mission

"BAMM BVI adheres to holistic development systems that promotes equitable synergies... constantly seeks to improve its productivity by learning and investing in various technological equipment, and training its employees..."

Proposed Mission

"Our mission is to be a leading provider 1 of sustainable quarrying and construction solutions 7 in Central Luzon 3. We are committed to delivering high-quality infrastructure projects 2 that meet modern engineering and environmental standards 6. Through continuous investment in advanced technology 4 and comprehensive employee training 9, we enhance efficiency and maximize our clients' resources 5. We foster equitable synergies among stakeholders 6, working towards long-term growth 5, environmental responsibility, and sustainable nation-building 10, ultimately paving the way for regional and national development 8."

PORTER'S FIVE FORCES
Industry Attractiveness Analysis

THREAT OF NEW ENTRANTS

MODERATE

PCAB licensing creates a barrier. However, the ₱9T Build Better More program attracts regional entrants.

POWER OF SUPPLIERS

WEAK

BAMM owns its quarry, eliminating reliance on local aggregate suppliers. Cement/fuel are global commodities.

COMPETITIVE RIVALRY

STRONG

PHILGEPS lowest-bid system intensifies price competition. Large AA nationals and aggressive local bidders.

POWER OF BUYERS

MODERATE

Government procurement rules lock in pricing. Private sector is highly price-sensitive but values reliability.

THREAT OF SUBSTITUTES

LOW

No viable substitute for physical infrastructure. Imported materials are highly cost-prohibitive.

CONCLUSION: The industry remains fundamentally attractive for efficient firms.

CPM KEY FACTOR 1
Quality & Project Excellence
Factor Weight: 17%
CompanyRating (1-4)Weighted Score
BAMM BVI30.51
Cavacon40.68
Alexi Louisse20.34
Comparison Metrics (Table 4.2)
MetricBAMMCavaconAlexi
On-Time Delivery95%97%90%
Client Satisfaction4.5/54.7/54.0/5
ISO CertificationNoneISO 9001:2015None
Competitive Context
  • BAMM possesses a pristine track record locally (zero structural defect claims, 95% on-time delivery across 10 years of municipal horizontal projects) but lacks formal ISO 9001 certification which Cavacon uses to win massive industrial deals.
Strategic Implication
  • Cavacon's ISO 9001 certification acts as an automatic filter for risk-averse multinational developers, making them the default winner for large-scale private projects.
  • BAMM must pursue ISO 9001 certification (Target: 2026) to formally codify its quality standard and close this credibility gap with private sector clients.
CPM KEY FACTOR 2
Financial Strength & Stability
Factor Weight: 15%
CompanyRating (1-4)Weighted Score
BAMM BVI30.45
Cavacon40.60
Alexi Louisse20.30
Comparison Metrics (Table 4.7)
MetricBAMMCavaconAlexi
PCAB LicenseCategory BCategory AAACategory A
Max Contract₱75M>₱225M₱150M
Current Ratio13.874.252.10
Competitive Context
  • BAMM actually leads the industry mathematically in liquidity (Current Ratio 13.87) and profitability (ROE 19.39%), but its absolute equity footprint (~₱39.5M) is dwarfed by Cavacon's scale, locking BAMM into PCAB Category B.
Strategic Implication
  • While operationally leaner and significantly more profitable than Cavacon per peso earned, BAMM's limited equity artificially restricts its bidding capability due to RA 9184 contract ceilings (Max ₱75M).
  • Aggressively accelerating equity accumulation to cross the ₱98.7M Category A threshold by 2027 is the central financial imperative — unlocking 4× the current contract ceiling.
CPM KEY FACTOR 3
Brand Reputation & Credibility
Factor Weight: 14%
CompanyRating (1-4)Weighted Score
BAMM BVI30.42
Cavacon40.56
Alexi Louisse20.28
Comparison Metrics (Table 4.4)
MetricBAMMCavaconAlexi
Years in Operation9 yrs20+ yrs12 yrs
Gov Projects Done324527
Market PresenceRegionalNationalLocal
Competitive Context
  • BAMM's brand equity is immense within the closed network of Aurora political and DPWH officials. However, outside of this niche geography, BAMM's brand awareness ranks essentially at zero.
Strategic Implication
  • Cavacon enjoys widespread national brand recognition, allowing them to easily execute multi-regional expansions into Central/North Luzon.
  • Building formal corporate identity — including a digital portfolio and ESG marketing materials — is essential before expanding into Nueva Ecija and Tarlac markets.
CPM KEY FACTOR 4
Market Reach & Project Portfolio
Factor Weight: 13%
CompanyRating (1-4)Weighted Score
BAMM BVI30.39
Cavacon40.52
Alexi Louisse20.26
Comparison Metrics (Table 4.6)
MetricBAMMCavaconAlexi
Coverage AreaAurora + CLNationwideAurora only
Client BaseLGU/DPWHGlobal/MixedLGU Only
Project DiversityLowHighestMedium
Competitive Context
  • BAMM relies 95% on government contracts constrained strictly to a single province (Aurora), while Cavacon executes highly diversified residential, commercial, and government projects multi-regionally.
Strategic Implication
  • This single-province and single-client dependence exposes BAMM to severe localized risks (e.g., local election budget freezes, localized typhoons).
  • Forceful revenue diversification into private resort and subdivision developers expanding into Baler and neighboring coastal towns is necessary to close this gap.
CPM KEY FACTOR 5
Equipment & Technology Utilization
Factor Weight: 11%
CompanyRating (1-4)Weighted Score
BAMM BVI20.22
Cavacon40.44
Alexi Louisse30.33
Comparison Metrics (Table 4.3)
MetricBAMMCavaconAlexi
Fleet TrackingManualGPS/TelematicsBasic GPS
Project SystemsPaper-basedCloud ERPExcel-based
AutomationLimitedHighModerate
Competitive Context
  • BAMM manages an impressive armada of heavy machinery completely manually (no GPS telematic tracking, no cloud PM, no ERP). Both Cavacon and Alexi Louisse use formal system tracking.
Strategic Implication
  • This manual tracking ceiling creates an estimated 15% budget overrun risk via hidden fuel inefficiencies, unauthorized equipment use, and delayed material hauling.
  • Investing in cloud-based project management and GPS telematics is the critical next step to unlock multi-site capacity scaling and close the gap with Cavacon.
CPM KEY FACTOR 6
Price Competitiveness
Factor Weight: 10%
CompanyRating (1-4)Weighted Score
BAMM BVI40.40
Cavacon20.20
Alexi Louisse30.30
Comparison Metrics (Table 4.5)
MetricBAMMCavaconAlexi
Internal SourcingYes (Quarry)NoNo
Material Markup0%15-20%12-15%
Inventory StatusHigh BufferLowLow
Competitive Context
  • BAMM fully owns and internally operates the Casiguran quarry, granting an exclusive 15–20% raw material cost discount compared to Cavacon and Alexi, who must purchase third-party aggregates at retail margins.
Strategic Implication
  • This represents BAMM's most potent competitive moat, guaranteeing they can underbid any competitor on RA 9184 (lowest calculated bid) infrastructure projects while maintaining a baseline 8% Net Profit Margin.
  • Expanding quarry capacity to deepen this price advantage — and eventually selling excess supply to competitors — is a key opportunity to compound this structural cost leadership.
CPM KEY FACTOR 7
Sustainability & Environmental Practices
Factor Weight: 10%
CompanyRating (1-4)Weighted Score
BAMM BVI40.40
Cavacon30.30
Alexi Louisse20.20
Comparison Metrics (Table 4.8)
MetricBAMMCavaconAlexi
DENR ComplianceFull IntegrityCompliantBasic
Rehab ProgramsActive/ReforestStatutoryMinimal
Carbon OffsetYes (Group)NoneNone
Competitive Context
  • BAMM natively aligns with the IDC corporate group's stringent ESG commitments, strictly adhering to DENR riverbed extraction limits and reforestation protocols.
Strategic Implication
  • As government procurement increasingly shifts toward 'Green Public Procurement' rules mandated by NEDA, BAMM will receive massive bid premiums over unpermitted extractors.
  • Publishing a formal ESG report and securing ISO 14001 certification will package BAMM's existing environmental compliance into an external branding and bidding advantage.
CPM KEY FACTOR 8
Workforce Expertise & Management
Factor Weight: 10%

MetricBAMMCavaconAlexi
Core Retention98%+82%75%
Safety RecordZero IncidentsModerateHigh Risk
Training/DevOn-the-jobISO FormalLimited
Competitive Context
  • BAMM boasts practically zero turnover among its core engineering team and strict DOLE safety certification compliance, fostering deep trust and cohesion.
Strategic Implication
  • This extreme cohesion allows BAMM to execute simultaneous remote projects in Aurora's mountainous terrains with virtually zero central direction or oversight from the owner.
  • Systematizing this cohesive team — through formal mentorship programs and hierarchical structuring — is critical to scaling concurrent project capacity.

EXTERNAL OPPORTUNITIES ANALYSIS BREAKDOWN
Strategic Growth Channels
Primary Strategic Drivers
O1: DPWH Infra Allocation Surge 14% Weight | 4 Rating
₱9T budget for "Build Better More" provides a massive horizontal infrastructure pipeline in Region III.
Strategic Context: Matches expertise in road/bridge concreting.
O3: Regional Aggregate Demand 10% Weight | 4 Rating
Construction boom in Aurora/Nueva Ecija drives 15%+ consumption growth, favoring quarry owners.
Strategic Context: Vertical integration captures retail margins.
O2: Flood Control Requirements 7% Weight | 3 Rating
Climate vulnerability dictates specialized drainage and seawall projects in coastal Aurora.
Strategic Context: Requires resilient historical track records.
Secondary Considerations
O4: Private Sector Expansion
Tourism surge creates design-build demand for resorts and commercial facilities. (5%)
O5: Sustainability Standards
Procurement shift toward "Green" bidding favors BAMM's reforestation legacy. (3%)
These factors support the core strategy by ensuring operational stability and material cost leadership.
EXTERNAL THREATS ANALYSIS BREAKDOWN
Market & Macro Risks
Primary Strategic Drivers
T1: PCAB Category B Project Ceiling 15% Weight | 1 Rating
₱75M single-contract cap prevents bidding as prime for major regional infrastructure complexes.
Strategic Context: Hard-caps revenue scale despite operational capacity.
T2: Rising Global/Local Input Costs 13% Weight | 2 Rating
Fixed-price government contracts provide no buffer for sudden diesel, cement, or steel price surges.
Strategic Context: Directly compresses net profit margins post-award.
T3: Political & Procurement Delays 12% Weight | 2 Rating
95% revenue dependence on public funding makes firm sensitive to election spending freezes.
Strategic Context: Can disrupt cash flow for up to 6 months.
Secondary Considerations
T4: Skilled Labor Out-Migration
Brain drain to urban centers/Overseas; need formal training pipelines to maintain depth. (11%)
T5: Regulatory Enforcement (DENR)
Tightening ECC/ISAG renewal standards threatens vertical integration cost moats. (10%)
These factors support the core strategy by ensuring operational stability and material cost leadership.

INTERNAL STRENGTHS ANALYSIS BREAKDOWN
Operational Foundations
Primary Strategic Drivers
S1: Vertical Integration (Quarry) 15% Weight | 4 Rating
15-20% raw material cost savings via internal extraction; bedrock of price leadership advantage.
Strategic Context: Moat against unintegrated local competitors.
S2: LGU Relationships & Trust 12% Weight | 4 Rating
10+ years of municipal trust in Aurora; built-in referral network lowers project acquisition cost.
Strategic Context: Reduces need for large formal sales teams.
S3: Liquidity (Current Ratio 13.87) 10% Weight | 3 Rating
Strong working capital enables rapid mobilization without high-interest debt/bridging loans.
Strategic Context: ROE of 19.39% reflects high efficiency.
Secondary Considerations
S4: Project Quality Track Record
Zero major structural defect findings; consistent ahead-of-schedule project turnovers. (8%)
S5: Experienced Workforce Cohesion
Loyal engineering team enables decentralized execution in remote mountainous Aurora. (8%)
These factors support the core strategy by ensuring operational stability and material cost leadership.
INTERNAL WEAKNESSES ANALYSIS BREAKDOWN
Scale & Efficiency Barriers
Primary Strategic Drivers
W1: Limited Capitalization (Equity Cap) 20% Weight | 1 Rating
₱39.5M Equity vs ₱98.7M Category A requirement; restricts bidding on nation-level tiers.
Strategic Context: Binding constraint on multi-year growth plans.
W2: Centralized Decision-Making 8% Weight | 2 Rating
Owner bottleneck slows response times during peak project overlap; lacks decentralized PMO.
Strategic Context: Inefficient resource allocation across parallel sites.
W3: Limited Technological Adoption 8% Weight | 1 Rating
Manual inventory and fleet tracking; hidden 15% leakage in fuel/hauling inefficiencies.
Strategic Context: Prevents data-driven margin optimization.
Secondary Considerations
W4: Weak Marketing & Branding
Invisible to tier-1 private resort developers; reputation currently provincial-only. (6%)
W5: 95% Government Dependency
Hyper-sensitivity to policy shifts and political budget freezes; requires diversification. (5%)
These factors support the core strategy by ensuring operational stability and material cost leadership.

SUMMARY OF SWOT MATRIX
Strategic matching of internal factors with external environment (Table 6.5)
SWOT Analysis
Strengths (S)
  • S1. Vertical Integration & Cost Efficiency — Quarry Ownership
  • S2. Strong LGU Relationships & Local Govt Reputation
  • S3. Strong Liquidity & Sustained Profitability (CR 13.87)
  • S4. Project Quality & On-Time Delivery Record (Sustained)
  • S5. Experienced Workforce & Organizational Cohesion
Weaknesses (W)
  • W1. Limited Capitalization — Constrains PCAB Category Upgrade
  • W2. Centralized Management Structure & Limited Delegation
  • W3. Limited Technological Adoption (No digital PM/GPS)
  • W4. Weak Marketing Strategy & Corporate Branding
  • W5. Heavy Dependence on Government Contracts (~95%)
Opportunities (O)
  • O1. DPWH Infrastructure Allocation Surge (2025–2027)
  • O2. Rising Demand for Climate-Resilient Infrastructure
  • O3. Increasing Demand for Quarry Aggregates in Central Luzon
  • O4. Private Sector Expansion in Aurora/Neighboring Provinces
  • O5. Shift Toward Sustainable / Green Construction Practices
SO Strategies
  • SO1. Bid for larger DPWH projects beyond Aurora
  • SO2. Leverage LGU ties for climate-resilient contracts
  • SO3. Expand quarry capacity for regional demand
  • SO4. Expand private sector construction
WO Strategies
  • WO1. Form strategic JVs for larger projects
  • WO2. Invest in digital project management
  • WO3. Expand marketing to neighboring provinces
  • WO4. Diversify to reduce govt reliance
Threats (T)
  • T1. PCAB Category B Ceiling Restricting Large-Scale Access
  • T2. Rising Input Costs Compressing Fixed-Contract Margins
  • T3. Political Transitions Disrupting Government Project Releases
  • T4. Skilled Labor Out-Migration Constraining Project Capacity
  • T5. DENR Enforcement Risks to Quarry Operations
ST Strategies
  • ST1. Use quarry to mitigate material costs
  • ST2. Pre-position bid packages for delays
  • ST3. Command premium pricing via quality
  • ST4. Implement workforce retention
WT Strategies
  • WT1. Strengthen financial risk mgmt
  • WT2. Decentralize decision-making
  • WT3. Enhance workforce development
  • WT4. Improve environmental compliance

Strategic Position: BAMM is in a high-growth market but currently lacks the massive scale of national players.
The priority: Aggressive investment in resources to move horizontally into Quadrant I.

QUANTITATIVE STRATEGIC PLANNING MATRIX (QSPM)
Evaluating Strategy Alternatives

Evaluating three major strategic alternatives arising from the matching stage.

Strategy AlternativeTotal Attractiveness Score (TAS)Rank
Strategy 1: Market Penetration (Aggressive DPWH Alignment) 6.61 #1 (Selected)
Strategy 2: Market Development (Private Sector Expansion) 5.84 #2
Strategy 3: Product Development (Climate-Resilient Infra) 4.98 #3
Why Market Penetration Won (TAS 6.61):
  • Capitalizes perfectly on the O1 (DPWH Budget) opportunity and S2 (LGU Relationship) strength.
  • Lowest capital requirement relative to revenue scaling, allowing faster equity accumulation for the PCAB Category A upgrade limit (W1).
  • Highest immediate impact on Retained Earnings over the 2025–2027 short-term timeframe.
STRATEGY 1: STRENGTHEN DPWH & LGU CAPTURE
Market Penetration Action Plan
Strategic Objective

Increase BAMM BVI's share of government infrastructure contracts in Aurora and Central Luzon by strengthening bidding capability, maintaining compliance standards, and deepening LGU relationships.

Expected Outcome

Revenue from government contracts grows from ₱93M (2024) to ₱255.4M by 2028, representing a 25–32% CAGR driven by disciplined bidding and the PCAB Category A upgrade in 2027.

Key Action Plans
ActionTimelineBudgetKPI
Assign dedicated bid prep team for govt contractsQ1 2025InternalWin rate ≥ 35%
Maintain PCAB/PhilGEPS permitsQ1 2025₱150K/yrZero Lapses
Develop pre-positioned engineering designsQ2 2025₱500K5 shovel-ready designs
Formalize LGU relationship managementQ1 2025₱100K/yr≥ 3 repeat LGU clients
Pursue PCAB Category A capitalization2025-2027InternalNet worth hits Cat A limit
STRATEGY 2: VERTICAL INTEGRATION
Quarry Supply Advantage Action Plan
Strategic Objective

Maximize cost efficiency by supplying internal construction projects with BAMM-owned quarry aggregates, while expanding third-party aggregate sales to regional contractors.

Expected Outcome

Internal material savings reduce cost of services by 2–3%; third-party aggregate revenues contribute ₱5–8M annually by 2027.

Key Action Plans
ActionTimelineBudgetKPI
Increase quarry extraction capacity by 15%Q2 2025₱3M capex15% volume increase by Q4
Establish long-term 3rd-party supply agreementsQ3 2025Internal₱5M+ third-party revenue
Implement digital inventory tracking for aggregatesQ3 2025₱200KZero stockouts
Optimize logistics routing to project sitesQ2 2025InternalFuel costs down 10%
Proactively renew MGB/DENR permitsQ1 2025₱200K/yrZero permit lapses
STRATEGY 3: COMPETITIVE DIFFERENTIATION
Quality & Sustainability Action Plan
Strategic Objective

Leverage BAMM's track record of zero-defect delivery and environmental stewardship to command premium bid pricing based on value differentiation.

Expected Outcome

Achieve ISO 9001 certification by 2026, enabling access to larger DPWH contracts supporting a 3-5% bid premium.

Key Action Plans
ActionTimelineBudgetKPI
Document and publish project completion portfolioQ1 2025₱100KUsed in all bids
Implement ISO 9001:2015 prep auditQ2 2025₱500KGap analysis produced
Pursue ISO 9001:2015 Certification2026₱1.5MCertification achieved Q2
Formalize sustainability reporting (ESG Brief)Q3 2025₱100KESG brief in all govt bids
Increase EHS compliance budget for quarriesQ1 2025₱300K/yrZero DENR violations
STRATEGY 4: PRIVATE SECTOR EXPANSION
Market Development Action Plan
Strategic Objective

Leverage existing expertise and quarry advantage to enter private sector segments and the specialized design-build flood control market.

Expected Outcome

Generates ₱4.7M in incremental revenue by 2026, growing to ₱9.7M by 2028, reducing DPWH dependency from 95% to 80%.

Key Action Plans
ActionTimelineBudgetKPI
Hire 2 licensed civil hydraulic engineersQ1-Q2 2025₱2.4M/yrDeployed by Q2 2025
Acquire engineering design software (Civil 3D)Q2 2025₱300KSoftware acquired & trained
Pursue pilot design-build flood control projectsQ3 2025Internal≥ 1 design-build contract won
Partner with engineering consultancy for tech backstopQ2-Q3 2025Proj-basedPartnership finalized
Develop climate-resilient design standards/templatesQ3 2025InternalUsed in all bids
STRATEGY 5: EQUIPMENT MODERNIZATION
Technology-Driven PM Action Plan
Strategic Objective

Invest in modern heavy equipment and digital project management tools to reduce downtime, improve resource efficiency, and enhance execution capabilities.

Expected Outcome

Reduces project downtime by 15%; digital management improves cost monitoring accuracy. Supports gross margin expansion to 34% by 2028.

Key Action Plans
ActionTimelineBudgetKPI
Complete fleet needs assessmentQ1 2025InternalPriority List 2025 done
Acquire priority heavy equipment (excavators, trucks)Q2 2025₱6MOperational by Q3 2025
Install GPS tracking on all fleet unitsQ2 2025₱400K100% GPS coverage
Deploy cloud-based PM platformQ2 2025₱250KAll projects integrated
Conduct digital skills training for project teamQ3 2025₱150K100% PMs/Foremen trained
STRATEGIC & FUNCTIONAL OBJECTIVES
The Roadmap to PCAB Category A
PRIMARY STRATEGIC OBJECTIVE
Upgrade to PCAB Category A by 2027
Requires achieving exactly ₱98.7M in Net Worth (Total Equity). Current: ~₱39.5M. Gap to close: ₱59.2M via Net Income.

FINANCIAL OBJECTIVES

  • Achieve ₱255M Annual Revenue by 2028 (25% CAGR).
  • Sustain Net Profit Margin >8% and ROE >15%.
  • Accumulate ₱59.2M in retained earnings by EOY 2027.

CUSTOMER / MARKET OBJECTIVES

  • Convert 3 DPWH exploratory bids into awarded contracts (2025).
  • Shift revenue mix to 80% Govt / 20% Private by 2028.

INTERNAL PROCESS OBJECTIVES

  • Achieve ISO 9001:2015 Certification by 2026.
  • Achieve ISO 14001:2015 Certification by 2027.
  • Maintain project overrun rate below 5% via digital PM tools.

LEARNING & GROWTH OBJECTIVES

  • Train and promote 3 Site Supervisors to Project Managers by 2026.
  • Achieve 100% cloud PM system adoption across all active sites.

The BSC Framework

Causal Logic: The gold arrows represent lead indicators driving lag results. Successful Learning empowers Internal Efficiency, which optimizes the Customer Experience, ultimately achieving Financial Sustainability.

  • Radial Arrows: Strategic alignment from central vision to each perspective.
  • Clockwise Loop: The sequential flow of tactical interdependency.

Strategic Victory: 2027 Upgrade Milestone

As shown in the table above, by the end of 2027, the cumulative Net Income naturally compounds BAMM's Total Equity to ₱98.67M — per the Projected Balance Sheet (Table 8.13, p.170).

This organically crosses the ₱98.7M threshold mandatory for the PCAB Category A license, without external equity dilution, new loans, or JV agreements.
